etymology.description: semantic 石(石) (shí, "stone; rock") + phonetic 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") -> code
etymology.interpretation: In this character, 石(石) (shí, "stone; rock") provides the meaning component suggesting something solid, substantial, or foundational, like a block or unit. 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") is borrowed for its sound to give the pronunciation. The sense of 'code' can be associated as a foundational unit of communication or computation.
etymology.simplification: Simplified as 码, where the left component 石(石) (shí, "stone") is replaced by a simpler form 讠, which represents speech or language elements to reflect the coding aspect.

examples.chinese: 我需要你的邮政编码(我需要你的郵政編碼)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xūyào nǐ de yóuzhèng biānmǎ.

examples.chinese: 请告诉我网页的源代码(請告訴我網頁的源代碼)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng gàosù wǒ wǎngyè de yuán dàimǎ.
examples.english: Please tell me the source code of the webpage.

examples.chinese: 这个代码很复杂(這個代碼很複雜)。
